locale.label: English
recent.title: Recently played
recent.subtitle: What you have played on your speakers
recent.empty: Nothing played yet. Start a station, playlist or track and it will show up here.
multiroom.heading: Multi-room and stereo pair
multiroom.intro1: Play the same source on two or more speakers in sync, or pair two speakers as a stereo set.
multiroom.bullet1: Group play across speakers on the same network.
multiroom.bullet2: Stereo pair: left and right channel across two speakers.
multiroom.bullet3: Live status from the desktop app.
multiroom.feedbackPrompt: Coming soon. Comments and ideas welcome on the tracking issue.
multiroom.betaNote: Experimental beta. If you have two or more speakers running ST Reborn, try grouping them: pick a main speaker, start playing something on it, then add the others. Afterwards please use Save logs at the bottom of the window and send them so I can see what the speakers did.
multiroom.needTwo: You need at least two speakers running ST Reborn to make a group.
multiroom.formed: Group created. The main speaker streams to the others. If they stay silent, make sure the main speaker is actually playing, then send logs.
multiroom.previewNote: For multi-room you need at least two speakers running ST Reborn on the same network.
multiroom.noOtherSpeaker: No other ST Reborn speaker found yet.
multiroom.modeMirror: Mirror (each speaker plays the same stream)
multiroom.modeHelp: Native lets the speaker firmware sync the group (best when it works). Mirror has each speaker pull the same stream itself (works more widely, may not be perfectly in sync). Try both and send logs.
multiroom.stereoNote: Use two SoundTouch 10 speakers as a real left/right stereo pair, each playing its own channel. STR drives the speaker's built-in pairing directly, so no Bose account is needed. Only the SoundTouch 10 can do this. To undo a pair (including one made in the Bose app), use Ungroup above. This is new, so please send logs.
multiroom.stereoSamePicked: Pick two different speakers for left and right.
multiroom.stereoFormed: Stereo pair created. Give it a few seconds. If a speaker stays silent or both play the same channel, send logs.
multiroom.pickHint: Pick the main speaker (the star), then tap the others to add them to the group.
multiroom.pickAtLeastOne: Tap at least one other speaker to add it to the group.
multiroom.updateWarn: Some speakers run an older ST Reborn. Open each one and use its update banner to get the latest before grouping, otherwise grouping may not work.
multiroom.formedN: Group active: {{n}} speaker(s) joined. If one stays silent, check it is on and the main speaker is playing.
multiroom.formedMirror: Mirror started for {{n}} speaker(s). If one stays silent, make sure the main speaker is playing, then send logs.
multiroom.formedNone: The main speaker accepted the request but reports no group members yet. Try Mirror mode, hit Refresh, or update the speakers, then send logs.
spotify.heading: Spotify Connect
spotify.intro1: Pair the speaker once in the Spotify mobile app, then send any Spotify track to it from your phone.
spotify.bullet1: Use the speaker as a Spotify Connect target.
spotify.bullet2: Works alongside the existing STR radio features.
spotify.bullet3: Re-pairing instructions in the STR app once stable.
spotify.feedbackPrompt: Experimental. If Spotify already works or fails on your speaker, please share details on the issue.
spotify.versionNote: Requires the latest STR version on the speaker.
spotify.works1: Send any Spotify track or playlist from the app to the speaker (Spotify Premium).
spotify.works2: Save playlists to the soft and hardware preset keys; a preset recalls shuffled, from the start, under its own account.
spotify.works3: The remote's next and previous track keys skip the song.
spotify.works4: Several household accounts: a preset recalls under the account that saved it.
spotify.limit1: The display may show "Spotify" instead of the playlist title, and a hardware press can briefly flash "service unavailable" before playback starts.
spotify.limit2: Switching between two accounts restarts playback for a few seconds and works best when the other account's app is not actively connected.
spotify.limit3: Thumbs up/down on the remote are not supported.
spotify.feedbackNote: Feedback very welcome, what works and what does not:
library.title: Library
library.subtitle: Browse music servers on your network (FRITZ!Box, Synology, Plex, miniDLNA) and play tracks on your SoundTouch.
library.loading: Looking for music servers and folders...
library.noServers: No music server found on the LAN. Make sure your FRITZ!Box, NAS or other DLNA server is powered on and reachable.
library.pickServer: Pick a music server above to start browsing.
library.capped: Showing the first {{n}} tracks. Use the search to find a specific one.
library.assignTitle: Save track to which preset key?
library.errorNoURL: This item has no playable stream URL.
library.formatMaybeUnsupported: "{title}" did not start on the speaker. It may be a file format the speaker cannot play (for example high-resolution FLAC, 24-bit or above 48 kHz). A standard-quality version usually works.
banner.sshRecommend: Remove the USB stick from the speaker and restart it once. Otherwise the speaker is attackable on the network.
topbar.title: Your SoundTouch speakers on the network
speaker.choose: Pick a Bose speaker above to control it.
speaker.emptyTitle: No speaker found on the network.
speaker.emptyHelp1: If the stick is already in the speaker and the Wi-Fi LED is lit, the stick agent may have hung. Briefly unplug power from the speaker and plug it back in.
speaker.emptyHelp2: If no stick is plugged in yet, set one up first.
speaker.rebootConfirmBody: Have you removed the USB stick? Otherwise SSH stays open after the reboot. Current playback will be interrupted.
speaker.rebootingToast: Speaker is restarting. It will be available again shortly.
speaker.stockTooltip: Stock Bose firmware. STR brings back internet radio and presets on this speaker.
speaker.stockConfirmTitle: Bring this speaker back to life with STR?
speaker.stockConfirmBody:
{{label}} still runs the stock Bose firmware. A one-time USB stick installs STR directly on the speaker. After that you control it entirely from this app: internet radio, presets, multi-room.

Open the USB stick setup now?
toast.sourceUnavailable: {{src}} is not available on this speaker
battery.label: Battery
preset.speakerUnreachable: Speaker is not reachable right now. Try again in a moment.
preset.searchHint: Search a station below, then assign to a key
preset.longPressHint: hold = save the current station here
preset.noCurrentStation: No station is playing right now, so long press has no effect.
play.errBoxStarting: speaker is still starting, try again in a moment
search.heading: Find stations
search.noBoseStations: No Bose-compatible stations in the results. Disable "Bose-compatible only" to also see HTTPS streams.
search.checkOk: Station was online at the last check
search.checkBad: Station was NOT reachable at the last check
search.favEmpty: No favorites yet. Tap the star on a station to add it here.
search.addStationHint: Missing a station? You can add it at radio-browser.info; it appears here after a short while and can be saved to a preset.
search.streamBlocked: This stream is blocked or geo-restricted in your region.
search.streamUnreachable: This stream could not be reached right now.
search.streamHls: This station uses a format the speaker cannot play yet.
search.tryingAlternative: {{name}} did not play, trying another source...
search.allSourcesFailed: No working source found right now. Please try another station.
update.speakerRunningOld: The speaker runs version {{boxVersion}}, the app is on {{appVersion}}.
update.rebootNote: Updating restarts the speaker. It is briefly unavailable and reconnects on its own; the app verifies the new version automatically.
update.uploadedToast: Update uploaded. The speaker reboots and takes up to a few minutes to answer again.
update.rebooting: Speaker is rebooting and reconnecting (this can take a few minutes)...
update.oldBuildWait: Speaker still answers the old build, {{remaining}} left...
update.tookLongerToast: Speaker takes longer than expected. Check power / network.
update.failed:
Update failed: {{err}}

If the speaker no longer answers, briefly unplug power and plug it back in.
update.stickInBody:
The USB stick is still in the speaker. An update will be undone on the next reboot, because the speaker re-installs the (older) version from the stick.

Pull the USB stick out of the speaker first, then update.
update.weakWifiBody:
The speaker's Wi-Fi signal is weak. An update downloads over Wi-Fi, and a weak link can interrupt it and leave the speaker half-updated.

Move the speaker closer to the router (or the other way round) for a stronger signal, then update. Update anyway?
settingsView.title: Speaker settings
order.name: Name
settings.languageHelp: App language. Independent from the radio search country.
credits.title: Open source & credits
credits.intro: STR is built on open source. Thank you to the projects it bundles, links, or builds on:
footer.saveLogsHint: Bundle the app log + per-speaker state into a zip you can attach to a GitHub issue. IPs and device IDs are anonymized.
footer.donateSlogan: Do you like the app? I'd appreciate a coffee.
speaker.rebootGenericBody: The speaker will restart in 1 second. Current playback will be interrupted. Continue?
speaker.rebootConfirmDetailedBody:
Have you removed the USB stick?

Stick still in → SSH stays open after the reboot.
Stick removed → SSH is closed after the reboot.

Current playback will be interrupted.
signal.notReported: Wi-Fi connected (signal not reported by this model)
settingsView.noSpeakerFoundTitle: No speaker found on the network.
settingsView.noSpeakerFoundHelp: To edit speaker settings, a prepared ST Reborn stick must be present. Prepare a stick first.
settingsView.stockBoxHelp: {{name}} runs the stock Bose firmware. There are no settings to edit until STR is installed. Open the USB stick setup to flash it.
settingsView.retryIn: Next try in 4 seconds, {{remaining}} attempts left.
settingsView.speakerDeadHelp1: Multiple attempts failed. Likely cause: the agent on the speaker died (for example after an OTA update that did not complete cleanly).
settingsView.speakerDeadHelp2: Fix: Unplug the speaker completely from power, wait 10 seconds, plug it back in. Once it is back on the Wi-Fi it should reappear here.
settingsView.errRefused: The speaker refused the connection. This happens briefly after a rename or right after waking from standby.
settingsView.errTimeout: The speaker is not answering right now. It might be on standby or not yet back on Wi-Fi.
settingsView.errBoseBusy: The speaker's Bose app is still starting or busy and sent no data yet. Try again in a moment.
settingsView.errNoRoute: The speaker is not reachable on the network. Check that it is powered on and on Wi-Fi.
settingsView.namePlaceholder: Type a room or pick from the list
settingsView.nameHelp: Appears in Bose apps, mDNS and UPnP discovery. A short speaker ID ({{uid}}) is added only if another speaker would otherwise have the same name.
settingsView.bassHelp: 0 is the factory default. Negative values reduce bass, positive values boost it (device dependent).
settingsView.langHelp: Experimental. Only English and Danish are confirmed. If you pick an unknown value and the speaker switches to a language you recognise, please report it on a GitHub issue so the list can grow.
settingsView.clockHelp: Experimental. The time zone is taken automatically from this PC so the speaker shows local time. Not all SoundTouch models have a clock display. Speakers that do not support it will show 'not supported' above.
settingsView.resumeOnPowerHelp: On by default. When you switch the speaker on, it starts the last station again, the way Bose did. If the speaker wakes by itself (for example as part of a stereo pair), it stays silent. Turn this off if you want silence when you switch the speaker on.
settingsView.airplayOptHelp: Keeps the AirPlay coprocessor awake so AirPlay starts faster and stays reliable. Only on speakers with AirPlay hardware (Portable, ST20). Changing it restarts the speaker.
settingsView.airplayOptRecommend: On: faster, more reliable AirPlay, a little more power. Off: deeper standby and longer battery, AirPlay reacts a bit slower. Tip: leave it On if you use AirPlay; turn it Off on a battery-run Portable you rarely stream to.
settingsView.airplayOptConfirmBody: Changing AirPlay optimization restarts the speaker so it can apply the setting (the same as the Bose app). It will be briefly unavailable. Continue?
settingsView.airplayOptSavedToast: Saved. The speaker is restarting to apply it.
settingsView.urlPresetHeading: Play a custom URL from a preset
settingsView.urlPresetHelp: Assign an audio URL (for example an MP3) to a preset key. Pressing the key, on the speaker or in the app, plays it. The URL must be reachable on your network and start with http (the speaker does not support https). Pressing it replaces what is currently playing.
settingsView.urlPresetInvalid: Please enter a preset and a valid http:// or https:// audio URL.
settingsView.webhookHelp: Advanced: the remote's thumbs-up and thumbs-down keys fire this HTTP request, for example a smart-home on/off toggle. Both keys send the same request, so use it as a toggle. It is stored on the speaker and keeps working without this app. Leave this closed if you do not need it.
settingsView.webhookModePresetNote: Replace works only if you also clear the STR preset for this key.
settingsView.webhookModeAuxPowerNote: Additional only: the webhook fires alongside the original function, which cannot be suppressed.
settingsView.wlanNotConnected: Speaker is not connected to Wi-Fi.
settingsView.wlanSwitchHint: Moving the speaker to a different Wi-Fi network? You can do it here, no Bose app needed.
settingsView.wlanPickPlaceholder: - pick a Wi-Fi or type one below -
settingsView.wlanRefreshTitle: Reload Wi-Fi list from this PC
settingsView.wlanPassPlaceholder: Wi-Fi password (leave empty for an open network)
settingsView.wlanWarn: On most speakers a wrong password simply rolls back to the previous Wi-Fi. On the Portable the speaker reboots to apply the change, so a wrong password there needs the USB stick to recover.
settingsView.wlanConfirmBody: The speaker switches immediately to {{ssid}}. With the wrong password it loses the connection. Continue?
settingsView.wlanSwitchedToast: Wi-Fi switched. The speaker is getting a new IP, search the list again in a moment.
settingsView.wlanRebootingToast: Applying the new Wi-Fi. The Portable reboots for this and is back in a few minutes, then search the list again.
settingsView.spotifyHint: Spotify Connect cannot be enabled without the Bose cloud. A Spotify Web API integration is on the roadmap.
settingsView.airplayHint: AirPlay 2 is present in hardware but only enabled by the original Bose setup with a Bose account. If the speaker was never paired with a Bose account, it stays inactive.
settingsView.regionHelp: Used as the default country for the radio search and language filter. Takes effect immediately and is stored on the stick.
settingsView.syncHardwareKeysHelp: If the preset buttons 1 to 6 on the speaker stop responding, this action sends all preset assignments to the speaker again.
settingsView.saveLogsHelp: Bundle the app log and per-speaker state into a zip you can attach to a GitHub issue or email to str@sichtbar-app.de. IPs and device IDs are anonymized.
settingsView.emailSupportHelp: Saves the diagnostic logs and opens a ready-to-send email to STR support (str@sichtbar-app.de). Just attach the saved file and describe your problem.
settingsView.emailSupportToast: Email opened. Attach the saved logs file before sending: {{path}}
settingsView.copyPresetsHelp: Copy the 1 to 6 preset assignment from this speaker to another one on your network, so you do not have to set up every speaker by hand.
settingsView.copyPresetsConfirmBody: This replaces presets 1 to 6 on {{target}} with the presets from this speaker.
settingsView.rebootHelp: Restart the speaker. Use this when you have just plugged in a freshly prepared USB stick and want the speaker to pick up the new settings now.
settingsView.trueFactoryResetHelpShort: Clears the Wi-Fi, device name and pairing so you can set the speaker up like new with the Bose app. STR itself stays installed.
settingsView.trueFactoryResetHelp: Bose's hardware factory reset (Preset 1 + Vol-) does not wipe the stored Wi-Fi profile, so the speaker auto-rejoins its last network within seconds and the Bose iOS app cannot re-onboard it. This option clears the stored profile, the Marge account state, and STR's NAND credentials, then reboots the speaker into a clean out-of-box state so the Bose iOS app sees a brand-new device.
settingsView.trueFactoryResetConfirmBody: {{name}} will lose its Wi-Fi profile, device name, paired Marge account, AirPlay home, and Spotify Connect bindings. STR itself stays installed. The speaker reboots into out-of-box state. Continue?
settingsView.trueFactoryResetDoneToast: Speaker wiped ({{n}} entries) and rebooting. Open the Bose iOS app to onboard from scratch.
settingsView.removeSTRBtn: Remove STR (speaker goes back to plain Bose)
settingsView.removeSTRHelp: Removes STR completely. Pull the USB stick out of the speaker first, otherwise it reinstalls STR on the next reboot.
settingsView.removeSTRConfirmTitle: Remove STR and return to Bose?
settingsView.removeSTRConfirmBody: {{name}} will be returned to a plain Bose speaker: the STR agent, its presets and settings are removed and the Bose network/account state is reset to factory. To use STR again you would re-install it from a stick. Pull the USB stick out of the speaker first, or Bose reinstalls STR on the next reboot. Continue?
settingsView.removeSTRDoneToast: STR removed ({{n}} items). The speaker is rebooting into plain Bose, wait about 60 seconds.
settingsView.removeSTRStickPresent: The USB stick is still in the speaker. Pull it out first, then try again, otherwise the speaker reinstalls STR from the stick on reboot.
fw.uptodate: Up to date. Bose has stopped releasing updates since the cloud shutdown. Last version {{version}} from Sep 2022.
fw.outdatedIntro: The last official Bose firmware for your model is {{version}} (Sep 2022). The agent runs reliably with that version.
fw.step1: The speaker must be on Wi-Fi. The stick can stay inserted during the update.
fw.step2: Open the Bose SoundTouch app on your phone (App Store / Play Store, if you still have it).
fw.step3: Pick the speaker. If an update is available, the app offers it.
fw.step4: Without the app: download the USB update tool from Bose, write the firmware to another stick, plug it into the speaker:
fw.hint: Note: Bose update servers may still be online in some regions even though the cloud streaming has shut down. If the app route fails, the USB tool is the most reliable path.
setup.heading: Prepare USB stick
setup.intro: Prepares a USB stick that you plug into your Bose speaker once. STR then runs from the speaker itself. Stick needs at least 4 GB.
setup.targetIntro: Choose which speaker the stick should set up.
setup.targetEmpty: No speakers discovered on the LAN yet. If yours is brand-new or just factory-reset, that is normal: pick "new or factory-reset speaker" below.
setup.targetCardFactoryHelp: For a new or factory-reset speaker not yet on Wi-Fi. After preparing, plug stick into speaker and power-cycle. Speaker reads Wi-Fi credentials from stick and joins your Wi-Fi automatically.
setup.targetMustPick: Please pick a target speaker first.
setup.goBtnFactory: Prepare USB stick for new speaker
setup.installWillTargetFactory: After boot from this stick, STR brings the speaker onto your Wi-Fi automatically, then installs.
setup.formatToggleLabel: Format stick first (FAT32, erases data). Recommended for new sticks. Windows asks for admin rights.
setup.step2Help: Type your own or pick a room. A speaker ID is appended automatically.
setup.langHelp: Shown on the speaker's display and voice prompts. Pre-selected from your country; change it if you like.
setup.step4Help: Speaker uses this on first boot. Deleted from stick after.
setup.formatWarnBody: Warning: STR is not yet installed on this stick. Preparing it will erase all existing data.
setup.noSticksFound: No USB sticks found. Plug one in (at least 4 GB) and click "Search again".
setup.stickFsHelp: The Bose speaker only reads FAT32. The stick will be reformatted during preparation (Windows prompts once for administrator rights). All data on the stick is lost.
setup.stickRefreshHint: You can still re-write the STR files to the stick with the update button below (e.g. to refresh run.sh after an STR update).
setup.alreadyConfigured: Already configured stick. If you want to reuse it (different speaker, new Wi-Fi or new name), the configuration below is rewritten to the stick on save.
setup.continueHint: The stick is already prepared. This jumps straight to installing on the speaker without writing it again.
setup.sessionPrepHint: Stick already prepared this session, no need to write it again.
setup.sessionPrepGo: Using the stick prepared this session.
setup.errFat32Required: The Bose speaker only reads FAT32. Enable the option Format the stick (FAT32) above and click Prepare again.
setup.eraseConfirmBody: There are no STR files on {{path}}. Preparing it will irreversibly erase all existing data. Continue?
setup.formattingLong: Formatting stick (FAT32). Windows will show an administrator prompt in a moment, please confirm with "Yes"...
setup.stickGoneAfterFormat: Stick no longer visible at {{path}} after format. Please select it again from the list above.
setup.nameSaved: Speaker name saved ({{name}}, speaker ID is appended on first boot).
setup.ejectedBody: Stick ejected. Plug it into the speaker's USB port. ST10: needs a micro-USB OTG adapter between the stick and the speaker.
setup.ejectFailed: The data is on the stick, but the automatic eject failed: <small>{{err}}</small> Please eject via the system file manager.
setup.waitingForBox: Waiting for the speaker to appear on your Wi-Fi, {{remaining}} left ...
setup.waitForBoxTimeout: The speaker did not appear within 5 minutes. Check that it is powered on, the stick is firmly inserted, and the Wi-Fi name and password are correct, then try again.
setup.boxFoundOnLAN: Found the speaker on your Wi-Fi ({{ip}}). Installing STR...
setup.alreadyInstalled: STR is already installed on the speaker at {{ip}} and running. A fresh install is not needed. To bring it to this app's agent version, use the button below.
setup.alreadyInstalledUpdateBtn: Update box agent to this build
setup.alreadyInstalledUpdateHint: Pushes the agent that ships with this version of the app to the speaker. Use this when the speaker is on an older build than the desktop app.
setup.installRunning: Running install.sh on the speaker via SSH, then rebooting. This takes about 2 minutes.
setup.repairSSHHint: Installs STR directly over the network when the USB stick cannot be read. The speaker reboots afterwards.
setup.repairSSHRunning: Repairing over SSH: copying STR to the speaker and installing, bypassing the stick. This takes a minute, the speaker reboots when done.
setup.skipToInstallBtn: Speaker already has the stick — install now
setup.skipToInstallHint: Use this if you already prepared a USB stick and plugged it into the speaker: it skips the stick steps and runs the STR install on the speaker directly.
setup.installBoxBusy: Speaker is reachable, but still finishing its start-up routine (system load {{load}}). Waiting until it has free capacity, then installing. Trying anyway in {{secs}} s.
setup.stickTooSmall: This stick is only {{gb}} GB. Please use a USB stick with at least 4 GB.
setup.stickNotWritable: This stick cannot be written to. It may be write-protected (check the small lock switch) or faulty. Please try a different USB stick.
setup.help.freshBoot: Start the speaker fresh: switch it off, insert the stick, switch it on, then wait 2 to 3 minutes (ST30, Portable and older models need a little longer) before you press Install.
setup.help.powerCycle: Power-cycle the speaker: unplug it for 10 seconds, plug it back in with the stick still inserted, wait 2 to 3 minutes, then refresh the speaker list.
setup.help.wifi: Check your Wi-Fi: a weak signal or a repeater can drop the connection to the speaker. Put the speaker close to the router and keep this computer and the speaker on the same network.
setup.help.stick: Check the stick: it must be FAT32 and at least 4 GB.
setup.help.stickInserted: Make sure the stick is pushed all the way into the speaker's USB port (the ST10 needs a micro-USB OTG adapter), then restart the speaker so it reads the stick.
setup.help.st30Port: On the SoundTouch 20 and 30, plug the stick into the large USB-A port on the back of the speaker, not the small micro-USB SETUP port (that one only shows the speaker as a network adapter and cannot read a stick). The ST10 needs a micro-USB OTG adapter.
setup.help.usbPicky: If no stick mounts at all on a SoundTouch 30, try a small plain USB 2.0 stick (4 to 32 GB). Avoid USB 3 sticks, large drives and SD-card adapters: the speaker's USB port gives limited power and can reject higher-draw media. If several low-power sticks still do not mount, the speaker's USB port may be faulty.
setup.help.logs: If it still fails, use Save logs at the bottom of the window and attach the setup.log from the stick to your report.
setup.helpLogsInstruction: If this keeps failing, use the button below to save the diagnostic logs and send them to str@sichtbar-app.de or attach them to your GitHub issue. The log is created even without a USB stick and now includes detailed speaker diagnostics.
setup.help.reformatApp: Re-prepare the stick here with the Format option ticked. The app formats it so the speaker can read it, which a manual format on a large stick often does not.
setup.help.smallerStick: If the stick is large (32 GB or more), try a smaller one (4 to 32 GB). Very large FAT32 sticks are often formatted in a way the speaker cannot read.
setup.help.differentStick: If it still fails, the stick may be faulty. Try a different USB stick, ideally a known brand.
setup.help.stickCopyFailed: The speaker could not copy the STR program from the USB stick into its own memory (a stick read error), so it never finished starting. Re-create the stick here, ideally on a different USB 2.0 stick, then push it firmly into the speaker's USB port before switching it on.
setup.help.usbPower: This is a power problem, not a faulty stick. The speaker's USB port could not keep the stick powered while reading it, so the stick dropped off during the install. The same stick normally works on a SoundTouch 10 or 20.
setup.help.lowPowerStick: Use a small, low-power USB 2.0 stick (4 to 16 GB). Larger or faster sticks draw more power than the speaker's USB port can supply.
setup.help.usbHub: Or plug the stick into a powered USB hub (one with its own power supply), so it gets steady power independently of the speaker's port.
setup.st30StickPowerWarn: SoundTouch 30 detected: its USB port can struggle to power some sticks, which causes a power error during install. Use a small, low-power USB 2.0 stick (4 to 16 GB), or connect it through a powered USB hub.
worldMap.inviteBtn: Share it on the world map
setup.stickLargeFat32: This is a large stick (over 32 GB). Large sticks are often formatted in a way the speaker cannot read. It will be reformatted to a compatible FAT32 during preparation; all data on the stick is lost.
setup.installDone: STR is installed and running on the speaker.
setup.installDoneHint: Switch to the Listen to music tab to use it. You can now safely remove the stick from the speaker.
setup.playHowStep1: In the Listen to music tab, put your radio stations or Spotify onto buttons 1 to 6.
setup.playHowStep2: Press that button (1 to 6) on the speaker itself to start playback.
setup.playHowBoseApp: The Bose SoundTouch app is no longer used for playback. If it says playback is not possible, that is normal because the Bose service was shut down, not a fault with your speaker.
setup.awaitBoxReadyTitle: Now do this on the speaker:
setup.awaitSearching: Looking for your speaker. After you plug the stick in and switch the speaker off and back on, it takes about a minute to appear. Keep this window open: the status updates here on its own. ({{remaining}} left)
setup.awaitSearchingStalled: Still looking for your speaker. Three quick things to check: the stick is pushed all the way in, the speaker is switched on, and the speaker uses the same Wi-Fi as this computer. I'll keep looking, so if you fix it now the status updates by itself. ({{remaining}} left)
setup.awaitSetupNetwork: Found a brand-new speaker that hasn't joined your Wi-Fi yet. It's showing its own temporary 'Bose SoundTouch' network. That's fine: the stick moves it onto your home Wi-Fi during setup, so you can continue now.
setup.awaitStillBooting: Found your speaker. It's just finishing starting up, this can take up to a minute. No need to do anything, the status turns green by itself.
setup.awaitBootedWithoutStick: Your speaker '{{model}}' is on your Wi-Fi, but it started up without reading the stick, so setup can't continue yet. This is the most common hiccup. Switch the speaker fully off, push the stick all the way in, then switch it on again. The moment it reads the stick, this turns green by itself.
setup.awaitFirmwareTooOld: Your speaker '{{model}}' is on your Wi-Fi, but it's running older Bose software ({{fw}}) that the setup stick can't work with. First update the speaker in the official Bose SoundTouch app, then switch it off and on with the stick in. Once it's up to date, setup continues here on its own.
setup.awaitFoundReady: Your speaker '{{model}}' is online and ready for setup. You can install ST Reborn now.
setup.awaitAlreadyStr: This speaker already has ST Reborn on it. You don't need to install again. You can continue, and if a newer version is available you'll be offered an update.
setup.awaitWrongSpeaker: There's more than one speaker on your Wi-Fi, and I'm waiting for the exact one you picked. The others are ignored on purpose, so nothing happens to them. Make sure the stick is in the speaker you chose. If you meant a different one, you can go back and pick it.
setup.awaitTimeoutWasOnNetwork: I could see your speaker on your Wi-Fi, but it never opened up for setup. Most likely the stick wasn't read at start-up. Switch the speaker fully off, push the stick all the way in, switch it on, then search again. If that still doesn't work, the speaker may need its Bose software updated first.
setup.awaitTimeoutNeverSeen: I couldn't find your speaker. Check that the speaker is switched on, the stick is pushed all the way in, and the speaker uses the same Wi-Fi as this computer, and that the Wi-Fi name and password you entered for the stick are exactly right. Then switch the speaker off and on and search again.
setup.awaitPrecondition: Leave the stick in the speaker during setup and don't switch the speaker off once it's ready. This window watches the speaker for you and unlocks the install button on its own.
setup.awaitStep1: Remove the stick from this computer.
setup.awaitStep2: Plug it into the speaker's USB port. Use whichever your speaker has: the large USB-A or the small micro-USB (the ST10 needs a micro-USB OTG adapter).
setup.awaitStep3: Switch the speaker off and on: unplug its power, then plug it back in. Leave the stick in. It takes 60 to 90 seconds to come up.
setup.awaitConfirmBtn: Speaker is ready, install ST Reborn now
setupAPPush.probing: Looking for a speaker in setup mode ...
setupAPPush.title: Set up Wi-Fi directly (experimental)
setupAPPush.instructionsBody: Experimental path that only signs the speaker in to your Wi-Fi. The recommended way is the USB stick: it does Wi-Fi and installation in one step. You can do this Wi-Fi sign-in just as well with the official Bose app (iOS/Android). A brand-new or freshly reset Bose speaker opens its own Wi-Fi network; ST Reborn briefly joins it and sends your home Wi-Fi credentials to the speaker.
setupAPPush.step1: Power on the speaker. It broadcasts a Wi-Fi network named 'Bose SoundTouch ...'.
setupAPPush.step2: Connect your PC Wi-Fi to that 'Bose SoundTouch ...' network. On Windows use the Wi-Fi list in the taskbar; on macOS use the Wi-Fi menu in the top-right corner.
setupAPPush.step3: Click 'Retry' below once your PC is connected to the speaker's Wi-Fi.
setupAPPush.foundBody: Pick your home Wi-Fi and click 'Send Wi-Fi'. The speaker then switches to your home Wi-Fi. This takes 1 to 5 minutes. In the meantime you can already switch your PC back to your home Wi-Fi.
setupAPPush.successHint: The speaker is now joining your home Wi-Fi. Switch your PC back to your home Wi-Fi and wait 1 to 5 minutes for it to appear in the list.
setupAPPush.unknownFailure: Push failed (no reason returned from speaker).
source.hint.airplay: Apple AirPlay 2. Activation depends on the original Bose setup flow.
source.hint.spotify: Spotify Connect. Not active without the Bose cloud.
source.hint.upnp: UPnP streaming. STR uses this actively.
source.hint.stored_music_media_renderer: Local media library on the LAN
setupAPPush.stickStillNeeded: This step only sends the Wi-Fi credentials to the speaker. Installing STR afterwards still needs the USB stick. For most people the stick alone is the simpler and more reliable way.
setup.stockKeepsWifi: Recommended: prepare the USB stick and plug it in. It adds STR and keeps your speaker's existing Wi-Fi, no reset and no going through the initial setup again.
spotify.nativeIntro: Spotify Connect works natively on your speaker. There is nothing to set up in this app: it uses your speaker's built-in Spotify.
spotify.nativeStep1: Open the Spotify app on a phone or computer on the same Wi-Fi as the speaker.
spotify.nativeStep2: Tap the “Connect to a device” (speaker) icon and pick this speaker.
spotify.nativeStep3: Play. Any Spotify account works; whoever connects last controls it. No login needed in this app.
spotify.nativeNote: Note: a Spotify Premium account is required (Spotify's own rule for Connect). The old “link an account in the Bose app” step is gone and not needed.
